title: Hockey stick $f$-divergences
abstract: In this paper we give a systematic and unified treatment and extensions of various results on a new notion of quantum $f$-divergences defined from quantum hockey stick divergences, the theory of which has been developed recently in \cite{BHT_fdiv,HircheTomamichel_integral,LiuHircheCheng2025}. In particular, we consider non-normalized states and hockey stick $f$-divergences defined from more general notions of quantum hockey stick divergences, as well as a somewhat more general form of the integral representation defined in terms of an additional real parameter. We also consider the extension of the theory to general von Neumann algebras, and extend various results from \cite{HircheTomamichel_integral,LiuHircheCheng2025} to this setting. Our main results here are the representation of the hockey stick $f$-divergences in terms of Neyman-Pearson error probabilities, which was given in the finite-dimensional case in \cite{LiuHircheCheng2025}, an extension of Jen\v cová's result \cite{Jencova2023} on the detection of reversibility of a quantum channel on a pair of states in terms of the hockey stick divergences, and an extension of a result in \cite{HircheTomamichel_integral} showing that the regularized hockey stick Rényi $α$-divergences coincide with the Petz-type Rényi divergences for $α\in(0,1)$ and with the sandwiched Rényi divergences for $α>1$. Moreover, we give some partial results on the characterization of when different notions of quantum $f$-divergences give the same value on a pair of quantum states.
comment: See the concurrent and independent work https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2607.05195 for the extension of hockey stick $f$-divergences to von Neumann algebras

bite.hook: A unified mathematical framework connects quantum hockey stick divergences to error probabilities and channel reversibility.
tldr: Extended the theory of quantum hockey stick f-divergences to non-normalized states and general von Neumann algebras.
Represented these divergences using Neyman-Pearson error probabilities and generalized channel reversibility detection.
Proved that regularized hockey stick Rényi divergences match Petz-type and sandwiched Rényi divergences depending on the parameter alpha.
bite.whyItMatters: Divergences measure how distinguishable two quantum states are, which is fundamental to quantum cryptography and information theory. This work unifies different mathematical tools, making it easier to analyze information loss in complex, infinite-dimensional quantum systems.
bite.eli5: Imagine trying to tell two very similar colors apart. A 'divergence' is like a mathematical magnifying glass that measures how different they are. This paper unifies several different types of magnifying glasses into one master toolkit, helping scientists study how quantum information gets blurred or preserved.

glossary.shortDef: A function that measures the difference between two probability distributions or quantum states.
glossary.eli5Def: A mathematical formula that tells you how easy or hard it is to tell two different setups apart.

glossary.shortDef: A symmetric algebra of bounded operators on a Hilbert space that is closed in the weak operator topology.
glossary.eli5Def: A highly advanced mathematical framework used to describe quantum systems with an infinite number of possible states.

glossary.shortDef: A generalized measure of information divergence that quantifies the distinguishability of states.
glossary.eli5Def: A flexible way to measure how much information is lost or how different two quantum states are, using a tunable parameter.
